message:
fix(audio): average by actual frame length when interleaving to mono

In `append_decoded_audio_to_mono` the interleaved-to-mono downmix was
dividing each frame's sum by `channel_count` — the expected channel count
for the whole buffer — rather than by `frame.len()`. That's equivalent
*only* when the input slice is an exact multiple of `channel_count`; if
the last chunk is short (partial frame from a truncated decode, or from a
caller that hands in a trailing remainder), `chunks()` still yields it
and we ended up dividing a 1- or 2-sample sum by the full channel count.
The result was a quiet, attenuated tail on the final chunk of any
not-perfectly-aligned buffer.

Using `frame.len()` makes the divisor match the number of samples
actually present in that chunk, so the average is correct for both
complete frames (`frame.len() == channel_count`) and any short tail
`chunks()` may emit. No behavior change for fully-aligned inputs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
